note: Canonical current-best pointer for the main4 constrained optimization track.

frozen_params.note: Phase28d hyper-fine sweep found a narrow feasible pocket near signal_scale 0.648-0.649; further changes require explicit b10/full re-validation

post_promotion_worstcase_check.scope: Top 5 lowest-slack scenarios identified from the reps=500 full-envelope tie-break
post_promotion_worstcase_check.interpretation: The promoted variant remains breach-free under tighter bootstrap on the previously identified worst-case rows. The reference keeps a wider minimum-slack cushion in that narrow subset, but the promoted variant preserves the higher mean test-relative return.

basin_findings.note: Phase28 deeper investigation: basin + focal + univariate + hyper-fine sweep
basin_findings.no_trade_band_cliff_note: ALL 4 perturbations (±1%, ±2%) breach Gate 055. v27_c04 sits at a precision-sensitive no_trade_band value.
basin_findings.hyperfine_note: Hyper-fine sweep found a narrow feasible pocket in the decreasing signal_scale direction; 0.648 survives full validation, reps=500 tie-break, and a targeted reps=1000 worst-case check.

next_phase: Phase35 bootstrap-stability audit confirms the Phase34 reps=200 breaches were Monte Carlo instability, not a new winner. The canonical reference is breach-free by reps=300 and exactly reproduces its Phase28d promotion metrics at reps=500, so governance in this thin-slack neighborhood should treat reps<300 as exploratory only and use reps>=300, preferably reps=500, for winner calls. Operational focus remains controlled external shadow then small-notional ramp with existing guardrails; research focus, if reopened, should pivot to a genuinely new mechanism family rather than more local threshold nudges around v28d_sig02.
